title: Rigor: Lemma Nrank(i) needs an explicit non-vanishing minor witness

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Rigor gap in Lemma `Nrank`(i): no explicit non-vanishing witness.**

The proof of $\mathrm{rank}\,N^{(\beta\gamma\delta)}=4$ for not-all-equal triples asserts that the $4\times4$ minor on columns $(1,1,1),(2,1,1),(1,2,1),(1,1,2)$ is "a sum of products of distinct $3\times3$ minors and can be made nonzero by a generic choice." No explicit specialization of $A^{(\beta)},A^{(\gamma)},A^{(\delta)}$ is exhibited, so the claim "not identically zero" is asserted, not proved (violates PROOF COMMANDMENT (3), gap-type F3).

The statement is TRUE (numerically $\mathrm{rank}\,N=4$ for every not-all-equal triple, all $A$ generic), but a complete proof needs an explicit witness. Suggested: choose rows so that $\kappa$ at four chosen index triples equals the four standard basis covectors $e_1,e_2,e_3,e_4$ (since $\kappa$ is a Plücker map and the rows can be specialized freely when $\beta\ne\gamma$), making the minor $=\pm1\ne0$, hence a nonzero polynomial.
